nl: The real requested release build path applies the required stack protector, fortify, PIE, RELRO, non-executable-stack, and overflow flags to compilation and final linking rather than only documenting them.
The final native executable builds and runs correctly, while readelf or equivalent concrete inspection confirms every binary hardening property required by the case.
The requested sanitizer configuration is executable in tests or CI, catches the supplied unsafe fixture when expected, and does not leak sanitizer flags into an incompatible production artifact.
Hardening changes use the task's actual Make, CMake, or compiler configuration and preserve clean builds under the supported compiler modes without unsafe library search paths.
